Actually, that wasn't the issue. There was a difference between partnering with organizations and welcoming individuals--a pretty continual issue in advocacy activities. Some of that has always been the case--women are more interesting to everyone, let's face it, even when it's not a control/freedom issue--and some of it is the result of the politics of the past near fifty years--The Culture Wars. The issue of bodily integrity is really personal to women(!), and having opposing organizations on that issue would likely have made the event contentious in ways that were not originally intended and did not occur. In other words, it was probably the right call to make at this time, although it may not be in the future.
